title: aulacocalyx subulata
intro: Aulacocalyx subulata, also known as the bushwillow, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial activities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues. Aulacocalyx subulata is often used in herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, to harness its therapeutic properties.
